          // Connect to the database

                       | inner_join<Post, "p">(Col<"user_id", "u">{} == Col<"user_id", "p">{})
                       | where(Col<"created_at", "p">{} >= "2025-12-01" && Col<"created_at", "p">{} <= "2025-12-31");

          // Specify the result type and execute
          auto result = (query | to<std::vector<ActiveUser>>)(conn);

          // Check for errors and return
          if (result) {
              return *result;
          } else {
              throw std::runtime_error(result.error().what());
          }
      }

#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace schema_migration_add_column {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct UserAfter {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::string phone_number;
  bool is_active;
};

/**
 * Function: Perform schema migration to add new columns.
 *

bool migration_add_column() {

  if (!conn_result) {

  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  const char* sql1 = "ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN phone_number VARCHAR(20)";
  const char* sql2 = "ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1";

  if (!execute_with_ignore(conn, sql1, "already exists")) {
    return false;
  }

  if (!execute_with_ignore(conn, sql2, "already exists")) {
    return false;
  }

  return true;
}

// Helper function to execute SQL and ignore errors containing the given substring
bool execute_with_ignore(const Ref<sqlgen::sqlite::Connection>& conn, const char* sql, const char* ignore_msg) {
  auto res = conn->execute(sql);
  if (!res) {
    std::string err = res.error().what();
    if (err.find(ignore_msg) != std::string::npos) {
      return true; // Ignore the error
    }
    return false; // Other error
  }
  return true; // Success
}

} // namespace schema_migration_add_column
```

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace analyze_social_network {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Friendship {
  int user1_id;
  int user2_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct FriendConnection {
  std::string user1_name;
  std::string user2_name;
};

std::vector<FriendConnection> analyze_social_network_impl(const std::vector<User>& users, const std::vector<Friendship>& friendships) {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();

  // Create tables if not exists
  (sqlgen::create_table<User> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)(conn);
  (sqlgen::create_table<Friendship> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)(conn);

  // Insert the data
  sqlgen::write(conn, users);
  sqlgen::write(conn, friendships);

  // Build the query for mutual friends
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<User, "u1">("username"_t1, "username"_t2)
      | sqlgen::inner_join<Friendship, "f1">("user_id"_t1 == "user_id1"_t1)
      | sqlgen::inner_join<Friendship, "f2">("user_id2"_t1 == "user_id1"_t2)
      | sqlgen::inner_join<User, "u2">("user_id2"_t2 == "user_id"_t2)
      | sqlgen::where("user_id"_t1 < "user_id"_t2 && 
          sqlgen::exists(
              sqlgen::select_from<Friendship, "f3">("user_id1"_t1 == "user_id"_t1 && "user_id2"_t1 == "user_id"_t2)
          ))
      | sqlgen::group_by("user_id"_t1, "user_id"_t2)
      | sqlgen::having(sqlgen::count("user_id2"_t1) > 0);

  // Execute the query
  auto result = query(conn).value();

  // Convert the result to FriendConnection
  std::vector<FriendConnection> connections;
  for (const auto& row : result) {
    connections.push_back(FriendConnection{
        .user1_name = std::get<0>(row),
        .user2_name = std::get<1>(row)
    });
  }

  return connections;
}

}  // namespace analyze_social_network
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/literals.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace column_is_null_checks {

struct Profile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
  std::optional<std::string> bio;
  std::optional<std::string> avatar_url;
};

struct Result {
  int profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
};

std::vector<Result> column_is_null_checks() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return {}; // Return empty vector on connection error
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();
  
  // Create the condition: bio IS NULL OR avatar_url IS NULL
  auto condition = ("bio"_c).is_null() || ("avatar_url"_c).is_null();
  
  // Execute the query
  auto result = (sqlgen::read<std::vector<Profile>> | sqlgen::where(condition))(conn);
  
  if (!result) {
    return {}; // Return empty vector on query error
  }
  
  // Transform the result to the desired type
  auto profiles = result.value();
  std::vector<Result> results;
  results.reserve(profiles.size());
  
  for (const auto& profile : profiles) {
    results.push_back(Result{
        .profile_id = profile.profile_id,
        .display_name = profile.display_name
    });
  }
  
  return results;
}

}  // namespace column_is_null_checks
```</answer>

 3. Then, we apply the `sqlgen::if_exists` modifier using the `|` operator to add the IF EXISTS condition.
 4. Convert the resulting object to SQL using `sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql`.
 5. Return the generated SQL string.

 According to the reference materials:
   - `sqlgen::drop<Log>` is a global variable of type `Drop<Log>`.
   - We can use the `operator|` to combine the drop object with `sqlgen::if_exists` (which is of type `IfExists`).
   - The `operator|` is defined to take an object of any type and an `IfExists` and returns a modified object with `if_exists_` set to true.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace drop_table_if_exists {

struct Log {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string message;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates dropping a table if it exists.
 * Returns: std::string.
 */
inline std::string drop_table_if_exists() {
    auto drop_stmt = sqlgen::drop<Log> | sqlgen::if_exists;
    return sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql(drop_stmt);
}

}  // namespace drop_table_if_exists
```</answer>
